stories.title: Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire
stories.description: Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.

stories.title: U.S., Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins House race
stories.description: The U.S. reached a last-minute ceasefire with Iran just before Trump's deadline for the country to meet his demands. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins the U.S. House race in Georgia.

stories.title: How Trump's threats to Iran have affected the global economy
stories.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Tomas {TAHM-us} Philipson, a former senior economic adviser in President Trump's first term, about the impact of the president's wartime threats on the global economy.

stories.title: A fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire shows cracks as attacks continue across the region
stories.description: Iran and the U.S. and Israel said they would suspend strikes but countries in the region continued to report attacks and Israel said it would not stop its assault in Lebanon.

stories.title: Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization
stories.description: President Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization and bomb its power plants by Tuesday night. Online, he said he agreed to suspend the bombing of Iran for two weeks.

stories.title: Congressional Democrats raise alarm over Trump's comments on Iran
stories.description: Dozens of congressional Democrats raised alarm Tuesday over President Trump's rhetoric about Iran. Most Republican lawmakers have been silent.

stories.title: Trump's deadline for an Iran deal looms. And, Artemis II crew begins the journey home
stories.description: In a press conference last night, Trump reiterated threats against Iran if the country doesn't accept a deal by 8:00 p.m. ET tonight. And, the Artemis II crew are on their way back to Earth.

stories.title: Why ceasefire deal with US has unsettled Iran's hardliners
stories.description: The two-week truce opens up the prospect of direct talks with the US but has angered Iran's hardliners, writes BBC News Persian’s Kasra Naji.

stories.title: Iran Strait of Hormuz warning adds to shipping uncertainty
stories.description: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

stories.title: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.description: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.title: More than 100 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.description: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.title: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.title: Oil prices plunge and shares jump on US-Iran ceasefire plan
stories.description: Crude prices tumbled by as much as 15% on the conditional pause but remain far higher than before the war.

stories.title: French couple leave Iran after more than three years in jail
stories.description: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris are on their way home after being allowed to leave the country, the French president says.

stories.title: Trump condemned over threat that Iran's 'civilisation will die'
stories.description: The UN's chief says he is "deeply troubled" by such statements, as the US president ramps up pressure on Iran to agree a deal to end the war.

stories.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

stories.title: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
stories.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

stories.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
stories.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

stories.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
stories.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

stories.title: US-Iran truce: ‘Sensitive behind-the-scenes conversations’ paved way for Pakistan-brokered ceasefire
stories.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Mumtaz Zahra Baloch, Ambassador of Pakistan to France and Permanent Delegate to UNESCO. According to Ambassador Zahra Baloch, this is less about victory in the conventional sense and more about the survival and recalibration of power. What we...

stories.title: Can ceasefire hold? US-Iran truce tested before talks start
stories.description: No apocalypse. Instead an eleventh hour ceasefire between Iran and the United States, one that is still being tested, two sides that are both claiming victory, and five and-a-half weeks of a war that may change the way the rest of the world looks at both Iran and the United State...

stories.title: Trump administration's claims of victory in Iran 'really quite hollow', analyst says
stories.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Jennie Shin, Barbara Slavin, Distinguished Fellow at the Stimson Center, says that "the Trump administration is desperately seeking a way to end the war and declare victory. The US can stop its attacks for a while but its claims of victory are really qui...

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel ready to 'return to battle at any moment' against Iran
stories.description: Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel remains prepared to confront Iran if necessary, despite a truce reached between the United States and Iran. Lebanon's civil defence service said 254 ​people had been killed in Israel's strikes across Lebanon on...

stories.title: ‘Unhinged, insane’: US lawmakers call for Trump’s removal via 25th Amendment
stories.description: Donald Trump may have announced a last-minute ceasefire agreement with Iran, but it’s done little to quieten the growing wave of Democratic lawmakers calling for his removal by invoking the 25th Amendment.

stories.title: Iran continues to target Gulf countries despite ceasefire announcement
stories.description: Reporting from Doha for FRANCE 24, Hoda Abdel-Hamid explains that Iran's demands regarding the control over the Strait of Hormuz and the departure of US combat troops from the region are unlikely to be acceptable to Gulf countries.

stories.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again as Israel pounds Lebanon, air defense systems go off in Tehran
stories.description: Air defense systems were activated in Tehran and in some areas of Alborz province in Iran less than 24 hours after the announcement of a ceasefire with the United States and Israel. FRANCE 24's correspondent in Tehran Reza Sayah says that "we are seeing the first signs of the fra...

stories.title: Israel pounds Lebanon with heaviest air strikes of the war
stories.description: Israel carried out its heaviest strikes on Lebanon since the ​conflict with Hezbollah broke out last month, even as the Iran-aligned group paused attacks on northern Israel and Israeli troops in Lebanon under a two-week US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: French pair held in Iran says not 'broken' after return to France
stories.description: Two French nationals who returned home Wednesday after being held for almost four years in Iran said they had never given up hope and would speak out about the "daily horror" of their Iranian prison. Cecile Kohler, 41, and Jacques Paris, 72, landed in Paris earlier Wednesday, wit...

stories.title: Hegseth hails Iran 'victory' as confusion remains over ceasefire deal
stories.description: US ​Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Wednesday ​that the United States has had a decisive military victory over Iran and that Tehran's missile ​program ‌has been functionally destroyed a day after the two countries agreed a two-week ceasefire to hold talks on ending the war...

stories.title: Israel pounds Beirut as Lebanon left out of ceasefire deal
stories.description: Lebanon's government said Wednesday Israeli strikes killed dozens and wounded hundreds across the country, with the capital Beirut hit by the most violent bombardment since the start of the war with militant group Hezbollah. It came despite a deal for a two-week ceasefire between...

stories.title: France to target Russia's 'shadow fleet' with tougher penalties for ships sailing under false flag
stories.description: France plans to double jail terms for owners and operators of ships that fail to fly the correct flag or refuse to comply with orders to stop, according to a draft update to the country's military planning law, in a move aimed at vessels suspected of transporting Russian oil in v...

stories.title: World reacts to ‘brutal’ Israeli attacks on Lebanon amid US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: Israeli attacks have killed at least 254 people and wounded 1,165 others, according to Lebanon’s Civil Defence.

stories.title: Iranians breathe a ‘ceasefire’ sigh of relief as all sides claim victory
stories.description: Celebration and scepticism coexist in Tehran as ceasefire offers reprieve despite doubts about its durability.

stories.title: White House says Trump’s ‘red line’ against Iran nuclear enrichment remains
stories.description: Trump's spokesperson says Vice President JD Vance will lead US team for negotiations with Iran in Pakistan on Saturday.

stories.title: Trump backs Israel, says Lebanon a ‘separate skirmish’ after ceasefire
stories.description: After Israeli strikes on Beirut, Donald Trump said Lebanon isn’t included in the Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: How Trump’s promise to ‘wipe out’ Iran could backfire on US power
stories.description: The world counts down to Trump’s threats of destruction.

stories.title: ‘A lot of work to do’ to reopen Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said more work is needed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: ‘Why my child?’: Yemen’s Taiz mourns sniper’s killing of teen
stories.description: Yemeni child killed by suspected Houthi sniper while heading to school in Taiz sparks grief and outrage among locals.

stories.title: Trump claims victory in Iran — but is this really a win?
stories.description: Before a single term has been negotiated, Trump is claiming total victory over Iran, but is this really a win?

stories.title: Lebanon PM welcomes US-Iran ceasefire as Israeli strikes hit Beirut
stories.description: Lebanon's PM Nawaf Salam expressed optimism in de-escalation hours after Tehran and Washington struck a ceasefire deal.

stories.title: How Pakistan managed to get the US and Iran to a ceasefire
stories.description: Backchannel talks led by Pakistan result in temporary ceasefire after weeks of fighting.

stories.title: Hegseth: Iran ‘begged’ for ceasefire, claims ‘historic victory’
stories.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth claimed a decisive victory over Iran.

stories.title: Trump says Lebanon not included in US-Iran ceasefire amid Israeli assault
stories.description: US president says war in Lebanon is 'separate skirmish' as Israel kills dozens across the country after regional truce.

stories.title: Vance warns Iran ceasefire hinges on good faith talks
stories.description: JD Vance says Donald Trump is seeking a deal “in good faith,” but Iran has to be willing to negotiate.

stories.title: Iran, US both claim victory, but did they actually concede ground?
stories.description: Those apparent concessions could become sticking points in upcoming talks in Islamabad.

stories.title: Hegseth touts US ‘victory’ over Iran as Tehran hails its own ‘historic’ win
stories.description: Defence chief declares total military victory over Iran, matching earlier victory claims from Tehran amid ceasefire.

stories.title: Israel pounds Lebanon with heaviest airstrikes of war as Hezbollah pauses attacks; US-Iran ceasefire hangs in balance
stories.description: Israel carried out its heaviest strikes on Lebanon since the conflict with Hezbollah broke out last month, even as the Iran-aligned group paused attacks on northern Israel and Israeli troops in Lebanon under a two-week US-Iran ceasefire. Consecutive explosions shook Beirut, sendi...

stories.title: China, Russia veto UN resolution to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Russia and China on Tuesday vetoed a draft resolution at the United Nations Security Council for reopening the Strait of Hormuz, while Pakistan abstained. The draft resolution prepared by Bahrain, which underwent six revisions during negotiations to bridge differences...

stories.title: First ships pass through Strait of Hormuz since ceasefire: monitor
stories.description: Two ships have passed through the Strait of Hormuz since Iran agreed to reopen the waterway as part of a ceasefire deal, maritime monitor Marine Traffic said on Wednesday. “The Greek-owned bulk carrier NJ Earth crossed the Strait at 08:44 UTC, while the Liberia-flagged Daytona Be...

stories.title: US, Iranian delegations expected in Pakistan on Friday; White House says first round of talks to be held on Saturday
stories.description: Less than 24 hours after the announcement of a ceasefire, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said on Wednesday that delegations from the United States and Iran would visit Pakistan on Friday for talks aimed at a peaceful settlement to the war in the Middle East. Iranian President Maso...

stories.title: North Korea fires two rounds of ballistic missiles: Seoul military
stories.description: North Korea fired multiple short-range ballistic missiles in two rounds on Wednesday, South Korea’s military said, after reporting an “unidentified projectile” was launched from the Pyongyang area the previous day. South Korean President Lee Jae Myung has sought to repair ties wi...

stories.title: Efforts to facilitate talks between US and Iran ongoing, Pakistani sources say
stories.description: Efforts to facilitate talks between the US and Iran are still ongoing, two Pakistani sources with knowledge of the discussions told Reuters on Tuesday, as US strikes on Iran intensified and US President Donald Trump’s deadline to unleash “hell” neared. One of the sources, a senio...

stories.title: Press review: Europe seeks to block US-Russia thaw as Russia eyes role in US-Iran talks
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Friday, June 6th

stories.title: Israel to continue striking Beirut until Lebanon disarms Hezbollah — defense minister
stories.description: "Agreements must be honored and the Lebanese government does not do what is required, Israel will continue to act, and with great force," Israel Katz said

stories.title: Orban sees EU bracing for another decade of countering Russia amid proxy war in Ukraine
stories.description: Commenting on the Ukraine crisis, the Hungarian PM said that Brussels viewed Ukraine as "the first line of European defense" where pan-European interests were currently being defended

stories.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah targets in Beirut suburbs — IDF
stories.description: Prior to that, the Israeli army issued a warning for this neighborhood, telling residents to leave their homes immediately

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
